document.docAuthor: Salani, Sula, Willenz, Philippe, Fernandez, Julio C. C. & Hajdu, Eduardo
document.masterDocTitle: Three new Hymedesmia Bowerbank, 1864 (Demospongiae, Poecilosclerida Hymedesmiidae) from the Southeast Pacific (Peru and Chile)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Three new Hymedesmia Bowerbank, 1864 (Demospongiae, Poecilosclerida Hymedesmiidae) from the Southeast Pacific (Peru and Chile)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Laboratório de Bentos, Instituto de Ciências Biológicas, Universidade de Brasília. Campus Universitário Darcy Ribeiro, Bloco E, s / n, Asa Norte, CEP 70910 - 900, Brasília / DF, Brazil. & Museu Nacional, Departamento de Invertebrados, Universidade Federal do Rio de Janeiro, Quinta da Boa Vista, s / n, CEP 20940 - 040, Rio de Janeiro, RJ, Brazil.

Royal Belgian Institute of Natural Sciences, Taxonomy and Phylogeny, Rue Vautier 29, B- 1000, Bruxelles, Belgium. philippe. willenz @ naturalsciences. be; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0003 - 4127 - 9346 & Université Libre de Bruxelles, Laboratoire de Biologie Marine, Avenue F. D. Roosevelt 50, B- 1050 Bruxelles, Belgium.

Museu Nacional, Departamento de Invertebrados, Universidade Federal do Rio de Janeiro, Quinta da Boa Vista, s / n, CEP 20940 - 040, Rio de Janeiro, RJ, Brazil. & Departamento de Genética (Dgen), Instituto de Biologia Roberto Alcântara Gomes (IBRAG), Universidade do Estado do Rio do Janeiro (UERJ), Rua São Francisco Xavier, 524 - PHLC-Sala 205, 20550 - 013, Rio de Janeiro, RJ.

Museu Nacional, Departamento de Invertebrados, Universidade Federal do Rio de Janeiro, Quinta da Boa Vista, s / n, CEP 20940 - 040, Rio de Janeiro, RJ, Brazil. & eduardo. hajdu @ gmail. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0002 - 8760 - 9403

subSubSection.paragraph:
Type species.
H. ( H. ) zetlandica Bowerbank, 1864
(by monotypy).

Diagnosis. Mostly thinly encrusting; ectosomal skeleton composed of smooth ectosomal tornotes strewn at the surface and forming bundles in the choanosome; choanosomal basal skeleton hymedesmioid, composed of one, two, or three sizes of acanthostyles standing singly erect on the substrate; microscleres, when present, are arcuate isochelae and/or sigmas (one species has microstrongyles). Chelae can occasionally form dense aggregations in the ectosome. Modified from van Soest (2002) .

Remarks. Van Soest’s (2002) diagnosis is amended here to account for the possible thickly encrusting habit, occurrence of a third category of acanthostyles, the possibility that chelae may form dense aggregations, and the rare occurrence of microstrongyles These characters can be observed from the new species described below.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 2.
Hymedesmia ( H .) santarositae
sp. nov.
A–B, live specimen (MNRJ 12843); C, skeleton architecture in transverse ground section; D, microstrongyles in ground section; E, choanosomal acanthostyle I; F, choanosomal acanthostyle II; G, ectosomal tornote; H–I, details of G; J, sigma; K–L, microstrongyles; M–N, isochelae. Scale bars: A, approx. 5 cm; B, 1 cm; C, 200 µm; D, 100 µm; E–G, 100 µm; H–I, 20 µm; J–N, 10 µm.

FIGURE 3.
Hymedesmia ( H .) peruana
sp. nov.
A, live specimen (MNRJ 13694); B–C, skeleton architecture in transverse ground section; D, choanosomal acanthostyle I; E, choanosomal acanthostyle II; F, choanosomal acanthostyle III; G, ectosomal tornote; H–I, details of G; J–K, isochelae. Scale bars: A, 2 cm; B–C, 200 µm; D–G, 100 µm; H–I, 20 µm; J–K, 10 µm.

FIGURE 4.
Hymedesmia ( H .) peruana
sp. nov.
Bar plot illustrating the size frequency distribution obtained by the Sturges Algorithm for the acanthostyles (n = 100).

TABLE 1.
Hymedesmia ( Hymedesmia )
spp. from Peru. Measurements (in µm) of the spicules (n = 30; unless stated otherwise in parentheses), length x thickness [as minimum– mean–maximum (± standard deviation)], distribution and bathymetry (in meters). Roman numerals correspond to size categories of acanthostyles.Abbreviations: Tor. (tornotes), Str . (strongyles), Sbty. (subtylotes), Tyl. (tyles), Sty. (styles), Ac. (arcuate chelae), Sig. (sigmas) and Mstr. (microstrongyles).

tr.th: MNRJ 12843―Holotype
MNRJ 13694―Holotype
MNRJ 12078―Holotype
MNRJ 12144―Paratype
IZUA-POR 175
MNRJ 9283
MNRJ 14875
MNRJ 14879

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 5.
Hymedesmia ( H .) humboldti
sp. nov.
A–B, live specimen (MNRJ 12078); C, skeleton architecture in transverse ground section; D, choanosomal acanthostyle I; E, choanosomal acanthostyle II; F, ectosomal tornote; G–H, details of F; I–J, isochelae. Scale bars: A, approx. 2 cm; B, approx. 0.5 cm; C, 200 µm D–F, 100 µm; G–H, 20 µm; I–J, 10 µm.

FIGURE 6.
Hymedesmia species
occurring in the southern hemisphere. Species (numbers): 1―
H. ( H. ) peruana
sp. nov.
; 2―
H. ( H.) santarositae
sp. nov.
; 3―

sp. nov.

H.(S.) australiensis ( Hentschel, 1911 )

; 34―
H. ( H. ) microstrongyla anisostrongyloxea Bergquist & Fromont, 1988
. Ecoregion (letters): a―Guayaq- uil; b―Humboldtian; c―Central Chile; d―Chiloense; e―Malvinas/Falklands; f―Uruguay-Buenos Aires Shelf; ―South; h―Antarctic Peninsula; i―Weddell Sea; j―East Antarctic Wilkes Land; k―Ross Sea; l―Kerguelen Islands; m―Namaqua; n―Western and Northern Madagascar; o―East African Coral Coast; p―Cargados Carajos/Tromelin Island; q―South India and Sri Lanka; r―Shark Bay; s―Leeuwin; t―Arafura Sea; u―New Caledonia; v―Three Kings-North Cape; w―Northeastern New Zealand; x―Central New Zealand.

TABLE 2. Species of
Hymedesmia
( H. ) Bowerbank, 1864 currently known from Antarctica, the sub-Antarctic region, the south-western Atlantic and south-eastern Pacific. First two pages: Measurements of spicules (in µm), length × thickness [either as minimum– mean –maximum, or minimum–maximum or common dimension (≈ mean)]. Following three pages, distribution and bathymetry (in meters), and schematic drawing or SEM images of spicules adapted from the literature. Roman numerals correspond to the size categories of acanthostyles. Abbreviations: n.r. (not reported from the original study), Ac. (arcuate chelae), St. (styles), Str. (strongyles), Sbt. (subtylotes), To. (tornotes), Uc. (unguiferate chelae).

th.emphasis: Species (color in situ / color in spirit)

tr.th:
Hymedesmia ( Hymedesmia )
H. ( H. ) antarctica Hentschel, 1914
orig. descr., as
H. simillima var. antarctica
( n.r. / yellow gray)
tr.td: 184–472 larger category with only spines on basal half and smaller category fully spined.

tr.th:
H. ( H. ) antarctica sensu Boury-Esnault & van Beveren (1982)
( n.r. / gray to beige)

H. ( H. ) barnesi Goodwin, Brewin & Brickle, 2012
orig. descr. (orange / white)

H. ( H. ) croftsae Goodwin, Jones, Neely & Brickle, 2014
orig. descr. (bright yellow / pale peach)

H. ( H. ) decepta Kirkpatrick (1907)
orig. descr.as
Myxilla decepta
( n.r. / reddish brown)

th.taxonomicName: H. ( H. ) decepta sensu van Soest (2002)

tr.th:
H. ( H. ) gaussiana Hentschel, 1914
orig. descr. ( n.r. / grey yellow)
tr.td: I. 256–312 × 16 II. 132–152 × 11 acanthostyles II are morphologically similar to the acanthostyles I

Hymedesmia
from South hemisphere. Coordinates (Lat./ long.) in decimal degrees.

subSubSection.paragraph: Legend: m- meters; * - when the article does not give the coordinate, it is measured at the midpoint of the location

heading.emphasis:
Identification key for
Hymedesmia spp.
from the Kerguelen Islands, South-eastern Pacific, South-western Atlantic, South Georgia and the Antarctic

paragraph.keyLead:
2a Ectosomal tornotes 500 µm long or bigger......................................
H. ( H. ) antarctica Hentschel, 1914
2b Ectosomal tornotes may occasionally approach 400 µm but usually are much smaller............................... 3
3a Largest acanthostyles approaching 500 µm long or bigger..................................................... 4
3b Largest acanthostyles around 400 µm long or smaller........................................................ 7

4b Microscleres include small unguiferate isochelae too..............................
H. ( H. ) decepta ( Kirkpatrick, 1907 )

6a Largest acanthostyles only marginally larger than 500 µm, no thicker than 25 µm, and smooth only close to the point...........................................................
H. ( H. ) mariondufresni Boury-Esnault & van Beveren, 1982

6b Largest acanthostyles over 600 µm, up to 40 µm thick, and smooth on the apical two thirds of the shaft.......................................................................
H. ( H. ) noramaloneae Goodwin, Berman & Hendry, 2019

7a Microscleres include forms (sigmas, microstrongyles) other than arcuate isochelae...........
H. ( H. ) santarositae
sp. nov.

8a Acanthostyles up to 250 µm only........................
H . ( H. ) laptikhovskyi Goodwin, Jones, Neely & Brickle, 2016

9a Arcuate isochelae larger than 30 µm (up to 37)...................................
H . ( H. ) gaussiana Hentschel, 1914

10a Stouter acanthostyles over 30 µm thick............................
H. ( H. ) barnesi Goodwin, Brewin & Brickle, 2012

11a Ectosomal tornotes; arcuate isochelae scattered, mean dimensions 15–16 µm..................
H . ( H. ) peruana
sp. nov.
11b Ectosomal strongyles; arcuate isochelae concentrated at the surface, mean dimensions 21–22 µm.................... 12

12a Live colour light orange (peach), arcuate chelae stout, and frequently nearly semi-circular, up to 27 µm.......................................................................
H . ( H. ) croftsae Goodwin, Jones, Neely & Brickle, 2016

12b Live colour reddish-orange (annatto-seeds), arcuate chelae slender, not semi-circular, up to 25 µm
H. ( H. ) humboldti
sp. nov.

14a Ectosomal styles (190–250 µm; acanthostyles I ≤ 360 µm, acanthostyles II ≤ 145 µm)......................................................................
H. (S.) lissostyla ( Bergquist & Fromont, 1988 )
, sensu
Bertolino et al . (2020)

15a Ectosomal oxeas (205–282 µm)...................................
H. ( S. ) pharos Goodwin, Brewin & Brickle, 2012

16b. Ectosomal strongyles (296–328 µm), acanthostyles I (352–480 x 25 µm, shaft only), acanthostyles II (128–144 x 9–10 µm).................................................................
H. ( S. ) hentscheli Van Soest & Hooper, 2020
